## [00:00 - 03:00]

Where were you at when you first joined the group financial? Our business was doing about 70k a month. Where are you at today? 145k cash, about 200k gross. And to be clear, your overall perspective on the inner circle, what's your initial take on being a part of this thing? My problems are small, and my problems are the same problems that a lot of other business owners have. I think too small, because oftentimes when you're alone as an entrepreneur, I feel like you're the only one that has that problem. Jumping in here, it was just so refreshing to see over 100 different entrepreneurs have very similar problems at a higher scale. So it gave me the assurance that like, yo, I can get to that scale. And that honestly, every business has problems. And problems are just problems, you're going to solve one, you're going to get to the next level, you're going to have to solve another. So it really calmed me down, it sat me back and allowed me to zoom out and have a bigger perspective. Did you watch our YouTube videos before you joined any of this? I watched every single video. I would put your video on my nightstand and just listen to it before I went to bed and just kind of like manifested everything you would say and say, hey, that's exactly what I'm going to do tomorrow. I'm not even joking, that's what I would do. Hey, some of you guys got to step up, you know, start playing videos on your nightstand, okay? Some people will tell us like, when they're coming through our paid advertising processes or they're coming through our own funnels, it just doesn't seem like you're holding things back, you know? Like your paid products, can they really be better than what's on YouTube? Yeah, I mean, on YouTube, you just get to listen to Jeremy talk, like paid product, you get to actually message him, you get to actually jump on two times a month calls, you get to be like one-on-one with pretty much every business owner in here who's solving the same problem, who put a lot of money to be in here, so everyone's willing to help each other out. And honestly, you get like 130 different versions of Jeremy at different stages and different types of companies. So it's not just Jeremy they're getting access to, it's a lot of people solving a lot of different problems at one time. You know, one of the entrepreneurs who did a million a month, he just gave me his whole game plan on like how he got here. He's coaching me up on my VSL, very successful entrepreneur, and he's also a one-on-one coach with me now. So I'm going to be messaging him my new VSL, he's going to be giving me feedback, and he's going to use his experience that took him to a million a month on me. So... Did he charge you for that? Absolutely not. Not crazy. So the masterminds are one piece of it, you got the weekly group calls, you got the twice a month one-on-one calls in the group chat. Do you get value out of one particular thing more than any other thing? All of them have their unique place in like the chain. It's like three months I'm grinding, and then I get to look forward to the mastermind where I come. I have a new problem, I come to the mastermind, I break through that problem, I go back to work for three months, I get to work with everybody kind of remotely, and in between that three months, I get to jump on with you to help me work through little nitty gritty details. And then so every little piece of your offer has something that, you know, is part of the chain. I'm a very direct person, you know, I hold people accountable, and we don't let you fuck yourself out of revenue potential. And we make sure we really, in layman's terms, are up your ass about getting stuff done in order to get more money. And we care about you, you know, we want you to get a strong ROI, of course. Do you feel that being a part of the group? Yeah, 100%. 100%, like I truly feel like Jeremy himself is like invested in my business. Like, I feel like he's low key a partner at sometimes, because he's like really obsessed with it, and sometimes more passion comes out than other times. But yeah, it truly feels like he wants me to win, and like it's not just for one reason or the other. He just wants to build a community of winners, and I think he's got this big vision of, I don't know, a billion dollars GDP out of this group.

## [03:00 - 03:10]

We're going to impact the global GDP with this group. What, a billion dollars GDP? So like Jeremy's on a big mission here, and like yeah, like he truly feels like an investor, and he's a partner in the business when he's really not.
